assertions.description: document.compatMode reports CSS1Compat (standards) or BackCompat (quirks); the change targets BackCompat documents.
getElementsByName is case-sensitive in all modes — used as a parallel surface that already matched what fragment navigation now does.
ID selectors are case-sensitive — the same rule fragment navigation now adopts in quirks mode.
A no-doctype iframe enters BackCompat mode, which is the document mode targeted by the Chrome 150 change.
Chrome 150+ finds the <a name> whose case exactly matches the fragment in BackCompat documents.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const major=Number((navigator.userAgent.match(/Chrome\/(\d+)/)||[])[1]||0);const beforeChrome150=major>0&&major<150;const f=document.createElement('iframe');f.hidden=true;document.body.appendChild(f);try{const d=f.contentDocument;d.open();d.write('quirks probelowermixedupper');d.close();await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,50));f.contentWindow.location.hash='Section';await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,80));const target=d.querySelector(':target');const actual=target&&target.getAttribute('name');return beforeChrome150?d.compatMode==='BackCompat':d.compatMode==='BackCompat'&&actual==='Section';}finally{f.remove();}})()

assertions.description: Chrome 150+ leaves a BackCompat fragment unmatched when no <a name> has the exact same case.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const major=Number((navigator.userAgent.match(/Chrome\/(\d+)/)||[])[1]||0);const beforeChrome150=major>0&&major<150;const f=document.createElement('iframe');f.hidden=true;document.body.appendChild(f);try{const d=f.contentDocument;d.open();d.write('quirks probelowermixedupper');d.close();await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,50));f.contentWindow.location.hash='SecTion';await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,80));const target=d.querySelector(':target');return beforeChrome150?d.compatMode==='BackCompat':d.compatMode==='BackCompat'&&target===null;}finally{f.remove();}})()

assertions.description: Element.id round-trips with original case (the DOM has always stored ID with original case; matching is what changed).
